mechanism: Pool worker is async but has no await before blocking spawnSync; settleItem awaits a worker that runs synchronously to subprocess exit, so runCappedPool admits lineages one-at-a-time. concurrency (read at :306) is inert; CLI fan-out is fully serial. Uncaught because fanout-pool.vitest makeGatedWorker (:35) yields via a gated Promise — the real spawnSync worker is never tested.
fix: Use async spawn, resolve on child close, keep timeout/kill; add an overlap test with two real sleeping stub subprocesses.

mechanism: Worker returns {exitCode,timedOut} (lines 359-360) and never throws on non-zero exit/timeout; settleItem marks it fulfilled, buildPoolSummary (:207-210) counts it succeeded, main (:373) emits status ok. fanout-merge skips unreadable registries as skipped_no_registry and mergeReviewRegistries with zero registries returns mergedVerdict PASS (:197-202) — a fan-out review where every lineage crashed yields a false PASS.
fix: Throw/mark-failed on non-zero exitCode or timedOut; make merge fail-closed when a requested lineage has no valid registry.

mechanism: buildLoopPrompt (:122,:131) synthesizes a natural-language instruction for the CLI executor instead of invoking the existing command verbatim, contradicting spec.md §2 Option-B 'each lineage runs the existing loop verbatim'. lineage.iterations is used only for timeout (:154-158), never forwarded as max_iterations. Parsing/validation/session-binding left to model interpretation — the divergence Option B was chosen to avoid.
fix: Invoke the existing command path per lineage with explicit executor+artifact-dir override+session_id+max_iterations, or amend spec §2 to accept prompt-synthesis for CLI kinds.

mechanism: Salvage writes iteration-${iterNum}.md unpadded (:106) from the raw integer state-log iteration number. If the canonical loop writes zero-padded iteration-001.md, recovered files miss downstream globs and salvage is defeated. Unpadded write confirmed from source; canonical pattern not re-read this session.
fix: Zero-pad to 3 digits (padStart(3,'0')) or pass the resolved iteration path into salvage.

mechanism: env: { ...process.env, ...extraEnv } forwards the full parent environment to every CLI lineage with no buildExecutorDispatchEnv allowlist; leaks unrelated provider secrets across executor kinds and weakens recursion/dispatch-stack guards.
fix: Build child env via buildExecutorDispatchEnv() and merge only lineage-specific vars.

mechanism: spawnSync options carry timeout (:344) but no detached/process-group setup; spawnSync timeout SIGTERMs only the direct child, so grandchild model processes (codex/claude/opencode) can survive a timed-out lineage.
fix: Use the audited async supervisor or set detached + process-group SIGTERM/SIGKILL escalation.

mechanism: tryReadJson swallows malformed registry JSON as null (:65); main filters registry!==null (:305) and reports it only as skipped_no_registry (:326), never a failure. A lineage with an active P0 but malformed registry is silently dropped from the merged review verdict — false-PASS contributor.
fix: Return a distinct parse-failure state and fail closed on malformed lineage registries.

mechanism: Research dedup keys on finding.id||finding.title (:97); review on finding.findingId||finding.title (:174). Both are lineage-local, not content hashes — identical findings across lineages with different local ids do not dedup (split attribution/inflated counts); unrelated findings sharing a local id can wrongly collide.
fix: Dedup by contentHash when present, else normalized file:line+title; merge sources and lineage attribution, keep highest severity.

mechanism: lineage count (:298) is positive with no upper bound and concurrency (:306) is positive-only; expandLineages (:381) materializes one entry per count, so a hostile/malformed count exhausts memory before the pool cap applies.
fix: Enforce a max count, max expanded-lineage total, and a max concurrency in parseFanoutConfig.

mechanism: fanoutConfigSchema (:304) is a plain z.object and parseFanoutConfig safeParses it (:323); Zod strips unknown keys, so a raw config carrying BOTH top-level executor AND fanout is accepted (executor silently dropped) instead of failing the documented EITHER/OR contract (comment :282-283). No root-level XOR guard exists.
fix: Add a top-level loop-dispatch validator that rejects both-present configs, called before YAML dispatch or fanout execution.

mechanism: recoveredText = extractTextFromOpencodeJson(savedStdout) is computed ONCE (:103) outside the per-iteration loop, then written (:120) into EVERY missing iteration file. Multiple missing iterations receive byte-identical whole-stdout content, corrupting per-iteration provenance and risking double-counting in convergence/merge.
fix: Recover from per-iteration logs or split stdout by iteration before writing each artifact; run post-dispatch validation and only count salvaged after the artifact validates.

mechanism: When DEEP_LOOP_TSX_LOADED!=='1' the script spawnSyncs a full copy of itself with the tsx loader (:53) and process.exits on its status (:65); a second top-level blocking spawnSync. Not the cause of C-01 but confirms a spawnSync habit and doubles per-run process startup; the C-01 async rewrite must target the inner post-tsx execution.
fix: Document the wrapper and ensure the async-spawn fix lives in the post-tsx execution path.

mechanism: buildAttributionMd computes each lineage row verdict from registry?.findingsBySeverity?.P0 (:242-247); if a per-lineage registry lacks findingsBySeverity the row silently shows PASS even with active P0s. Shape mismatch with the authoritative merge fields (activeP0/openFindings). Low blast radius (attribution doc only).
fix: Compute attribution verdict from the same fields the merge rollup uses (activeP0/openFindings).

mechanism: Cross-loop field-name split: research_auto.yaml branches on config.executor.type (4x, :618/:612/:766), review_auto.yaml branches on config.executor.kind (4x, :741/:735/:1009); both command docs WRITE config.executor.type; loader executor-config.ts canonicalizes type->kind and treats type as deprecated (:87-112). Research is self-consistent on the deprecated .type. Review reads .kind but the doc writes .type, so if the review YAML predicate reads the raw (non-loader-normalized) config, config.executor.kind is undefined and a plain native review run mis-branches (native skip/dispatch wrong). Real drift; P1, P0 if predicate reads raw config.
fix: Branch both YAMLs on config.executor.kind and normalize legacy type->kind before any workflow predicate, or make command docs write .kind. One canonical field end-to-end.

mechanism: All of 003/004/005/006 graph-metadata.json have status:planned (:35,:32,:32,:32) while each child spec.md has completion_pct:100; implementation-summary.md is ABSENT in all four children, so graph-metadata cannot derive complete. Parent spec.md continuity is also stale (completion_pct:33, next_safe_action Phase 003). 8 metadata/spec disagreements across a packet being reviewed as shipped.
fix: Add implementation-summary.md per child and regenerate graph-metadata; refresh parent continuity block.

mechanism: Canonical iteration_pattern is iteration-{NNN}.md (research_auto:108, review_auto:100; NNN = padStart(3,'0') per session-state-hierarchy.cjs:26) but fanout-salvage.cjs:106 writes unpadded iteration-${iterNum}.md. Mismatch breaks exact-{NNN} assert_exists checks (research_auto:860/917); the iteration-*.md wildcard reader (:994) is permissive. The salvage vitest itself uses unpadded names so it enshrines the bug.
fix: Zero-pad salvage filenames to 3 digits or pass the resolved iteration path; update the test to assert padded names.
